entries.title: How to use this guide
entries.body: - Search by keyword in this file or on the Guide UI at /guide. - Start with the section for your function area. - Follow endpoint references and runbooks for day-to-day operations.

entries.title: Detailed feature guide
entries.body: Core platform feature areas automated in this sprint: - Lead scoring and CRM routing with execution logs. - Outreach orchestration, sequencing, and suppression controls. - Onboarding context capture, brief generation, an

entries.title: Style guide
entries.body: Implementation style patterns used across all automation APIs: - App Router route handlers in src/app/api/admin/automation/**/route.ts. - Explicit requireAuth call first in every non-webhook API route. - Staff-only guard

entries.title: Marketing guide
entries.body: Marketing automation relies on: - Signal-aware pipeline reporting from status and trend runs. - Weekly KPI summaries for campaign-level visibility. - MBR and council prep documents for narrative-ready synthesis. Recommen

entries.title: Sales and outreach guide
entries.body: Sales-facing automation endpoints include: - /api/admin/automation/outreach/initial-sequences - /api/admin/automation/outreach/follow-up-timing - /api/admin/automation/outreach/classify-replies - /api/admin/automation/ou

entries.title: Customer tracking guide
entries.body: Customer tracking is powered by: - usage_monitor_runs - customer_health_checks - customer_status_reports - support_issue_triage - help_center_routing_logs How-to: 1. Run usage monitor and health checks first. 2. Generate

entries.title: Detailed code guide
entries.body: Primary code locations: - Automation APIs: src/app/api/admin/automation/ - Auth guard helper: src/lib/admin-automation-auth.ts - CRM scoring and runner: src/lib/lead-scoring.ts and src/lib/lead-scoring-runner.ts - Admin

entries.title: Operations guide
entries.body: Operational functions and how-to list: - Billing operations: - invoices-receipts - renewal-reminders - failed-payment-retries - revenue-recognition-inputs - refund-workflow-triggers - subscription-status-updates - paymen

entries.title: SRE guide
entries.body: SRE practices used: - Every automation endpoint is auditable via persisted runs. - Alerting is centralized and queryable. - Critical states are codified (failed, risk, critical, mismatch, degraded, late). - Admin monitor

entries.title: Testing guide
entries.body: Recommended checks after automation changes: - Type and lint checks for modified route handlers. - Endpoint-level smoke calls with dry-run options where available. - Data-layer checks for expected table insertions. - Ale

entries.title: Repository guide
entries.body: Repository structure relevant to automation: - src/app/api/admin/automation/: operational APIs - src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/admin/: admin UI - supabase/migrations/: schema and RLS evolution - docs/: operational and bu

entries.title: Database structure and schema guide
entries.body: Major migration milestones: - 096: lead scoring CRM foundations. - 097: Tickets 6-15 automation and execution logs. - 098: Tickets 16-29 onboarding/customer-ops/billing tables. - 099: Tickets 30-56 billing/revenue/engine

entries.title: Pricing and billing guide
entries.body: Billing automation supports: - Invoice and receipt run generation records. - Renewal reminder scheduling and send logging. - Failed payment detection and optional retry orchestration. - Plan and subscription status trans

entries.title: Revenue and finance guide
entries.body: Revenue operations support: - Revenue recognition input preparation. - Stripe/Supabase/accounting sync run snapshots. - Revenue classification by tier/segment payloads. - Payout matching summaries and exception report ge

entries.title: We Analyzed 300+ Executive Hiring Profiles. Here Is What Actually Gets Senior Leaders Interviewed.
entries.body: Most executive candidates still position around tenure and title. Hiring teams at the C-suite and VP level screen for transformation evidence, cross-functional influence, and business outcomes. Here is what the data says.

entries.title: Why Executive Recruiters Go Quiet (And How Senior Candidates Can Break the Pattern)
entries.body: Strong candidates are often ignored for reasons that have little to do with capability and everything to do with decision pressure. The frame you use in outreach can reduce that pressure and increase response rates.

entries.title: Why Starting Monday Exists: Executive Search Is Not a Resume Problem
entries.body: C-suite and VP searches are won on specificity, timing, and preparation quality. We built Starting Monday to replace generic templates with role-specific positioning and high-context execution.

entries.title: How We Estimate Early Role Signals (And Why We Use a 1-3 Week Window)
entries.body: A transparent look at our methodology: how we define event dates, posting dates, lag windows, and the primary sources we use to estimate early executive-role timing signals.
tags: how we estimate job posting lead time

entries.title: Executive Job Search Daily Routine: What the Top 1% Do Differently
entries.body: The strongest executive candidates run a disciplined daily operating rhythm: signals, targeted outreach, prep, and follow-through. Here is the exact routine and the weekly metrics that keep momentum high.
tags: how top executive candidates run search

entries.title: What Executive Search Firms Actually Want from C-suite Candidates
entries.body: Most technology executives think about executive search firms the wrong way. Here is how retained search actually works, what gets you on the short list, and why timing matters more than almost anything else.
tags: how to work with executive search firms

entries.title: How Long Does a C-suite Job Search Really Take?
entries.body: The honest answer most people do not say out loud: six to eighteen months. The range is that wide for a reason, and where you land in it depends on decisions you make before you start.
tags: how long does executive job search take

entries.title: How VPs of Technology Make the Move to the C-suite
entries.body: The VP-to-CIO move is not a promotion. It is a repositioning. What changes in the CIO seat, what search firms look for, and how to build the case before the search starts.

entries.title: We Scanned 600+ Career Pages in Q2 2026. Here Is What C-suite Hiring Looks Like Right Now.
entries.body: Real data from monitoring hundreds of company career pages across technology-intensive sectors. What the C-suite job market looks like in Q2 2026, which signals precede a search, and how to monitor career pages before roles appear on LinkedIn.
tags: how to monitor company career pages

entries.title: How to Prepare for a CISO Interview
entries.body: CISO interviews test something most security executives are not trained to do: explain risk in business terms to a room that does not think in frameworks. Here is how to prepare for the conversations that actually matter.
tags: how to prepare for CISO interview

entries.title: What Companies Actually Want in a Chief Data Officer
entries.body: Most CDO job descriptions tell you what the company wants you to do. Very few tell you what they actually need. The gap between those two things is where most CDO searches go wrong.
tags: what companies look for in CDO

entries.title: How to Negotiate C-suite Compensation
entries.body: The compensation conversation for a C-suite role has a different structure than every negotiation you have had before. What to know before the offer, how to read the room, and where most executives leave money on the table.

entries.title: How to Prepare for a Board Presentation as a New C-suite Executive
entries.body: The board presentation is not a technology briefing. It is a risk and value conversation. What the board actually wants to hear in the first ninety days, and how to walk in ready.
tags: C-suite presenting to board of directors

entries.title: What PE-Backed Companies Look for in a C-suite Technology Leader
entries.body: PE-backed companies hire C-suite leaders for a different reason than public companies do. The mandate is tighter, the timeline is compressed, and the equity is structured around an exit. What you need to know before you take the call.

entries.title: The Executive Resume Gaps That Kill C-suite Candidacies
entries.body: Most executive resumes are read by a search firm associate before they reach a partner. Here are the gaps that filter candidates out before the first conversation and how to close them.
tags: how to write a C-suite resume

entries.title: How to Work with Retained Search Firms Without Losing Leverage
entries.body: Retained search firms work for the client, not the candidate. Understanding how they actually operate changes how you manage every relationship and every search.
tags: how to work with executive recruiters

entries.title: How to Build Your Target Company List for an Executive Search
entries.body: The fastest C-suite searches start with a list. Not of job openings. Those are late. A list of forty to sixty companies you would say yes to before the offer is made. Here is how to build it, what to put on it, and what to watch for once you have it.
tags: executive job search target company list
how to find executive jobs before they post

entries.title: CIO vs CTO: Which Role Is Right for You?
entries.body: Most technology executives spend their career in one seat without seriously considering whether the other fits better. The CIO drawn to product strategy might be a natural CTO. The CTO frustrated by ambiguity might be a natural CIO. Here is how to tell.
tags: CIO or CTO which is right for me
should I be a CIO or CTO

entries.title: CTO vs VP of Engineering: Which Path Is Right for Your Next Move?
entries.body: Most technology executives reach for the title with more letters. That is the wrong frame. The real question is which role matches how you create value and what kind of work makes you excellent.
tags: VP engineering vs CTO career path
should I be CTO or VP Engineering

entries.title: How Long Does a CTO Job Search Really Take?
entries.body: Ask companies how long a CTO search takes and they will say 8 to 12 weeks. Ask candidates and the honest answer is very different. What determines where you fall in the range and what you can actually control.
tags: how long does CTO job search take
chief technology officer job search time
how long to find CTO job

entries.title: How to Use LinkedIn in an Active CIO or CTO Search
entries.body: LinkedIn is the only professional platform where your most important future employers spend time. Used well, it builds inbound interest without looking like you are searching. Used poorly, it is a credibility liability. Here is the difference.
tags: how to use LinkedIn for executive search

entries.title: How Do CIOs Actually Find New Jobs?
entries.body: Not job boards. Not LinkedIn Easy Apply. Not cold-emailing recruiters. Here is how CIO roles are actually filled and what that means for how to run your search.

entries.title: How to Build Your Target Company List for a CTO Search
entries.body: The CTO role has no agreed-upon definition. Before you can build a target company list, you have to decide which version of the CTO you are building toward. That decision shapes every other part of the search.

entries.title: The Search Has No Manager. Here Is How to Build One.
entries.body: On the day your CIO role ends, you become your own organization: a one-person company with no revenue, no runway, and no manager. Most executives are not prepared for this. Here is what changes when you treat the search like a business.

entries.title: What Executive Coaches Get Wrong About Their Clients' Job Searches
entries.body: Most executive coaches underestimate how much of the search happens outside the session. Here is what changes when your clients have the right infrastructure.
tags: how executive coaches help job search

entries.title: The Infrastructure Gap in Executive Career Coaching
entries.body: Most executive coaching practices are strong on strategy and weak on infrastructure. The gap shows up in first-round interviews, stalled pipelines, and sessions that spend too much time on context. Here is what changes when you fix it.
tags: how executive coaches improve client search

entries.title: The Job Search Changes When You Reach VP. Here Is How.
entries.body: Most of what you know about running a job search stops working at the senior executive level. The rules are different, the timeline is longer, and the preparation standard is higher. Here is what actually changes.
tags: how job search changes at VP level

entries.title: What CHROs Should Know About Technology Executive Career Transition
entries.body: Technology executive searches run longer and require different support than general executive outplacement. What CHROs can do to improve outcomes for CIOs, CTOs, and VPs of Technology in transition.
tags: how to support technology executives in transition

entries.title: Why Technology Executive Candidates Fail the First Round
entries.body: The most common reason a technology executive candidate does not advance past the first round is not fit. It is preparation. What adequate preparation looks like at the senior level, and what search firms can do about the gap.
